reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 4 (Exploitability): This finding passes as a protocol-correctness vulnerability. The issue is code-intrinsic (happens by design during fork transitions without attacker input), but it causes a protocol deviation affecting data availability during the critical Fulu fork activation window. updateMetadataV2ToV3 (eth2_network.nim:2170-2176) converts pre-Fulu altair.MetaData to fulu.MetaData but omits custody_group_count, leaving it at default 0. peerPingerHeartbeat (line 2220) refreshes metadata every 30 minutes based on elapsed time only, with no fork-awareness. All peers connected before Fulu will retain custody_group_count=0 for up to 30 minutes after fork activation. matchIntersection (request_manager.nim:377-384) uses this stale value to compute custody overlap, potentially blocking data column sync network-wide during the fork transition window. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-6a4369e9-inv-049 in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_MANUAL_15_109_190_216.json requires 'all peer metadata fields that are semantically redefined by the new protocol version must be refreshed from each connected peer within a bounded time. Stale pre-transition default values must not be used for post-transition decisions.' The code violates this requirement by using 30-minute fixed timer with no fork-awareness. Severity: Medium is appropriate per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json threshold for vulnerabilities affecting >5% of network. The 30-minute stale window during Fulu activation could affect the entire network's data availability sampling capability.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: After a protocol upgrade or fork transition, all peer metadata fields that are semantically redefined by the new protocol version (e.g., custody_group_count becoming meaningful at Fulu) must be refreshed from each connected peer within a bounded time. Stale pre-transition default values (e.g., zero) must not be used for post-transition decisions.
